title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
At a lavish tea party hosted by Tom Addison at the Doveton villa, Mr. Satterthwaite observes the guests and the setting. Tom, an aging man with gouty feet, welcomes the attendees, which include Air Force Captain Simon Gilliat and Beryl Gillingham, who is managing the tea service. 

Among the younger guests are two half-brothers, Roland and Timothy, both in their mid-twenties with red hair. Mr. Satterthwaite notes that Timothy has fair skin and a fine figure reminiscent of his deceased acquaintance, Lily. Also present is Inez, a beautiful girl of Spanish heritage and daughter of the retired Dr. Horton; she is Rowan's first cousin and lives in the adjacent house. Mr. Satterthwaite observes that both Roland and Timothy appear to have an interest in Inez.

As the party continues with sandwiches and colorful teacups, the conversation covers various topics, such as Timothy's nacre pipe from Germany. While observing the river and the nearby mill, Mr. Satterthwaite reflects on his past connections to the estate. He observes that Beryl Gillingham has taken the place of Simon's first wife, Lily; although no one physically resembles her, her presence is felt at the gathering. Additionally, Mr. Satterthwaite perceives a sense of tension between Tom Addison and Beryl.
